lead_signal.headline: US and Israel conducted airstrikes against Iran on February 28, 2026, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other central leaders.

conflict_updates.title: US-Israel airstrikes kill Iran's Supreme Leader

This represents a major military escalation in the Middle East theatre. Trajectory implies heightened risk of retaliatory actions and regional instability.

weekly_brief_narrative: US and Israel airstrikes on Iran killing Supreme Leader Khamenei on February 28 dominate as the week's signal due to decapitation of leadership and potential for retaliatory escalation across Middle East proxies. This event weakens Iranian support for allies like Assad, enabling HTS rapid overthrow in Syria. Russia-Ukraine holds at high baseline intensity as Europe's major war with no new doctrinal shifts. Gaza sees continued humanitarian deterioration per WHO report #5, with health crisis underscoring displacement persistence. Sudan remains among Africa's deadliest conflicts at major war scale, stable but with structural under-reporting risks. Myanmar sustains high fatality levels as Asian major war, no de-escalation evident. DRC eastern fighting locked in major status, mineral resources amplifying economic stakes. Iran tensions spike post-strikes, with diplomatic channels collapsed. Haiti gang crisis stable but weather events exacerbate vulnerabilities. Displacement velocity elevated in Gaza and African theatres, with WHO noting Middle East crisis; no new corridor openings. Sahel watches Syrian jihadist gains for momentum spillover. Monitor Iranian retaliation thresholds, HTS consolidation in Syria, and ACLED Tier 1 drops for Sudan/Myanmar/DRC to catch under-covered shifts. US diplomatic proposals de-weighted structurally. Potential triggers: Iranian proxy activations, Russian I3 rhetoric beyond baseline, or displacement surges past 5 on I6 scales.

theatres_with_thin_coverage: Sahel; Tier 1 gaps in ACLED/UCDP/OCHA for under-covered theatres; fewer than 3 Sudan/Myanmar/DRC findings flags structural gap.
